reasons: Franco can drag or suppress one enemy, giving Beatrix a confirmed target for weapon follow-up.
Beatrix's weapon damage becomes more reliable when the target has limited ability to move, dash, or interrupt her firing angle.

proof.summary: Franco confirms one target with hook or suppression, while Beatrix follows with the weapon best suited to the target's position.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a priority target inside Beatrix's damage range.
Beatrix has the right weapon ready before Franco commits.
Beatrix waits until the target is actually controlled before firing.
failureCases: Franco catches a tank instead of a priority target.
Beatrix is reloading or holding the wrong weapon.
Enemy peel interrupts Beatrix before the damage lands.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag one priority target, giving Beatrix a clean single-target damage window.
Beatrix can use Kaja's lock to aim or burst without relying on raw lane pressure or guessing the enemy movement path.

proof.summary: Kaja suppresses and pulls one enemy, while Beatrix uses the confirmed target window for weapon damage before the target can escape.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a squishy or high-value target.
Beatrix is already in range before Kaja commits.
Beatrix prepares the weapon that fits the target's final position.
failureCases: Beatrix is not ready with the correct weapon.
Enemy peel interrupts the follow-up after the suppression window.

reasons: Kalea can knock enemies airborne or move a grabbed target, giving Beatrix a controlled damage window.
Beatrix can punish Kalea's forced target position with a prepared weapon angle.

proof.summary: Kalea creates a displacement or airborne-control window, while Beatrix follows with weapon damage from a prepared angle.
worksBestWhen: Kalea moves the target toward Beatrix's safe damage angle.
Beatrix prepares the weapon before Kalea commits.
Kalea and Beatrix focus the same target.
failureCases: Kalea moves the target away from Beatrix's line of fire.
Beatrix is pressured before she can follow up.
The enemy uses peel or immunity after Kalea's setup.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies, giving Beatrix a teamfight damage window with the correct weapon choice.
Beatrix can stay behind the engage and punish the controlled area instead of starting the fight herself.

proof.summary: Atlas creates a grouped control window, while Beatrix converts the setup into weapon-based area or burst damage.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches multiple enemies or a high-value backline target.
Beatrix has the correct weapon ready before Atlas commits.
Beatrix keeps safe spacing and waits for the pull or control window.
failureCases: Atlas catches enemies outside Beatrix's weapon angle.
Beatrix is reloading or holding the wrong weapon.
Enemy divers force Beatrix to reposition before she can follow up.

reasons: Tigreal can group or hold enemies long enough for Beatrix to follow with weapon damage.
Beatrix can punish Tigreal's engage from behind the frontline when she has a safe angle.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates a crowd-control opening, while Beatrix follows with weapon-based damage if she has the right weapon and safe angle.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Beatrix has weapon and range ready.
Beatrix uses the correct weapon for grouped or single-target follow-up.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far from Beatrix.
Beatrix is forced to reposition during the setup.
The enemy spreads before Tigreal completes the engage.
